About

About the photographer

Hey! I'm an amateur astronomer and astrophotographer, who's been told constantly to share his findings and techniques, and so here we are. I hope you enjoy the content and find it useful.

This site records each session or project, and the techniques used to capture and process the images. I also share my thoughts on equipment, workflow, and other topics related to astrophotography. I photograph all kinds of objects, but am limited by the seeing conditions and light pollution in my area, so I mainly photograph bright objects like the moon and planets.

If you're interested in any of my techniques, gear, workflow, images, etc., there's an e-mail below. Clear Skies.

Equipment

Imaging stack

  • Tracking mount optimized for long exposures and polar alignment accuracy.
  • Wide-field refractor with corrected optics for sharp star fields.
  • Dedicated astro camera with calibrated darks, flats, and bias frames.

Workflow

Capture to final

  • Plan the target window, framing, and exposure length per session.
  • Capture calibrated subframes with consistent temperature and gain.
  • Stack, stretch, and refine color while preserving star shape and contrast.

Contact

Location: Based in the UAE
